morning_pages.notes: First side was just complaints about being cold. That's fine. By the third side I'd gotten to something real: the anxiety about whether Aliveness is different enough from Four Thousand Weeks. I wrote it out fully and by the end I think the answer is yes — FTW is about time; this is about the quality of engagement with time. But I still need to find the argument that makes that distinction feel surprising.

writing.main_project: Aliveness notes — tested three possible framings
writing.notes: Tried framing 1: 'clinging to control.' Too clinical. Tried framing 2: 'the problem of bracing.' Better — more physical, more embodied. Tried framing 3: 'the difference between being alive and merely living.' Too purple. Will keep working.

imperfectionist.response: Good response. One reply from a reader who said she'd been a completionist for 20 years and this was the first time she'd felt permission to leave something unread. That's the whole thing right there.

family.notes: Rowan wanted to know what my book is about. I said 'being really here instead of sort of here.' He thought about it and said 'like when you're on your phone?' I said yes, exactly like that. He seemed satisfied. Children are efficient.

atelic_activity: Paper pop-up work in the evening. Made a bad one. Made a slightly less bad one. Rowan helped.
blockers: Book framing still in search mode — not a crisis but requires patience
financial_note: Reviewed the Substack numbers. Steady. Not lavish but enough. The speaking fee from March will help.
